title: Documentation: README performance section update

files_forbidden: any source, component, route, scanner, test, message-catalog, or pipeline file

acceptance_criteria: The README performance section accurately describes the new behaviour: Lighthouse-accurate score (matches Google PageSpeed; the old double-penalty removed), Google verbatim real-user (CrUX) verdict shown alongside, best-practices grade, the explicit 'not measured' state on provider failure, the optional default-OFF desktop measurement, and the short-TTL cache (5 min) with re-scan bypass.
Any stale claim contradicting the new behaviour (e.g. old penalty wording, '15 security modules', mobile-only absolutes) in the touched section is corrected; do not rewrite unrelated README sections.
No code or behavioural files touched; markdown only; npm run lint passes (README changes do not break lint/build).
output_format: the README diff plus a one-line summary of what changed and why
